successor: IBM (division closed; product became NetVista then discontinued)
notes: IBM Network Computer Division (Route 100, Somers NY). IBM Network Station renamed NetVista in April 2000; NetVista thin client line withdrawn April 2002 with no replacement.
Aberdeen Group acquired by Harte-Hanks 2001; brand continues.
Oracle co-announced NC Reference Profile May 1996; Oracle NC (diskless computer) produced 1996-2000.
Sun announced JavaStation October 1996 as NC competitor; acquired by Oracle 2010.
Apple co-signed NC Reference Profile 1996; developed Mac NC concept (Pippin) which Steve Jobs killed in 1997. Apple pivoted to iMac.
Netscape co-signed NC Reference Profile; Netscape Navigator was key browser technology for NCs. Acquired by AOL for $4.2B in 1998.
NCD provided Windows terminal and X-terminal products; IBM resold NCD R-series after exiting X-terminal market 1996.
Wintel (Windows/Intel) paradigm was the NC's explicit target. Microsoft countered with NetPC specification and WinCenter Pro (cited in study).
